preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 62%, with South Africa a clear underdog at 13%. The gap of roughly 49 percentage points reflects a substantial difference in forecast strength between the two sides.

The draw probability of 25% indicates a meaningful likelihood of stalemate. The win-loss spread is wide enough that South Korea enters as a strong favorite, though the model does not rule out an upset; South Africa's path to victory remains open at better than 1-in-8 odds, while draws account for one quarter of possible outcomes. The match is expected to lean decisively toward one outcome rather than remain closely competitive.

The model and sportsbook consensus are broadly aligned across all three outcomes. South Korea's win probability differs by 3 percentage points, the draw by 1 point, and South Africa's by 4 points—all within typical variance between forecasting methods. Polymarket pricing sits slightly closer to the model on South Korea (62% vs 62%) while offering marginally lower draw odds. No material divergence suggests conflicting views of the match's fundamentals.
